courses.name: Calculus I
courses.description: Limits, derivatives, integrals, and applications of calculus.

courses.name: Calculus II
courses.description: Techniques of integration, sequences, series, and power series.

courses.name: Calculus with Proofs
courses.description: A rigorous treatment of single-variable calculus with an emphasis on proofs.

courses.name: Linear Algebra I
courses.description: Systems of linear equations, matrix algebra, vector spaces, and eigenvalues.

courses.name: Linear Algebra II
courses.description: Abstract vector spaces, inner product spaces, and diagonalization.

courses.name: Multivariable Calculus
courses.description: Partial derivatives, multiple integrals, vector calculus, and applications.

courses.name: Multivariable Calculus with Proofs
courses.description: Rigorous treatment of multivariable calculus. Inverse and implicit function theorems.

courses.name: Ordinary Differential Equations
courses.description: First and second-order ODEs, systems, Laplace transforms, and applications.

courses.name: Abstract Mathematics
courses.description: Introduction to mathematical proofs: sets, relations, functions, induction, and number theory.

courses.name: Algebra II
courses.description: Groups, rings, fields, and polynomial algebra.

courses.name: Analysis II
courses.description: Topology of R^n, compact sets, integration on manifolds, differential forms.

courses.name: Groups and Symmetries
courses.description: Group theory: subgroups, homomorphisms, quotient groups, group actions.

courses.name: Introduction to Mathematical Logic
courses.description: Propositional and predicate logic, completeness, compactness, and incompleteness.

courses.name: Introduction to Topology
courses.description: Topological spaces, continuity, connectedness, compactness, and metric spaces.

courses.name: Introduction to Graph Theory
courses.description: Graphs, trees, connectivity, planarity, colouring, and network flows.

courses.name: Complex Variables
courses.description: Analytic functions, Cauchy's theorem, residues, and conformal mappings.

courses.name: Introduction to Combinatorics
courses.description: Enumeration, generating functions, recurrence relations, and graph theory.

courses.name: Real Analysis I
courses.description: Measure theory, Lebesgue integration, and function spaces.

courses.name: Introduction to Physics I
courses.description: Mechanics, waves, and thermodynamics for life science students.

courses.name: Introduction to Physics II
courses.description: Electricity, magnetism, optics, and modern physics.

courses.name: Foundations of Physics I
courses.description: Classical mechanics with a focus on problem-solving and mathematical methods.

courses.name: Foundations of Physics II
courses.description: Electricity, magnetism, and electromagnetic waves.

courses.name: Practical Physics I
courses.description: Experimental methods, data analysis, error propagation, and lab techniques.

courses.name: Electricity and Magnetism
courses.description: Electrostatics, magnetostatics, Maxwell's equations, and electromagnetic waves.

courses.name: Thermal Physics
courses.description: Thermodynamics, statistical mechanics, and kinetic theory.

courses.name: Introduction to Quantum Physics
courses.description: Wave-particle duality, Schrödinger equation, hydrogen atom, and spin.

courses.name: Practical Physics II
courses.description: Advanced lab experiments in optics, atomic, and nuclear physics.

courses.name: Electromagnetic Theory
courses.description: Advanced electrodynamics, radiation, and relativistic electromagnetism.

courses.name: Advanced Classical Mechanics
courses.description: Lagrangian and Hamiltonian mechanics, oscillations, and canonical transformations.

courses.name: Quantum Mechanics I
courses.description: Formal structure of quantum mechanics, angular momentum, and perturbation theory.

courses.name: Nuclear and Particle Physics
courses.description: Nuclear structure, radioactive decay, particle physics, and the Standard Model.

courses.name: Introduction to Linguistics
courses.description: Phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, and language change.

courses.description: A survey of core areas in linguistics with emphasis on language universals.

courses.name: English Grammar
courses.description: Descriptive grammar of English: syntax, morphology, and usage.

courses.description: How the mind processes language: speech perception, word recognition, sentence parsing.

courses.description: The relationship between language and thought across cultures.

courses.name: Syntax I
courses.description: Introduction to syntactic theory and formal analysis of sentence structure.

courses.name: Phonetics
courses.description: Articulatory and acoustic phonetics, IPA transcription, and speech analysis.

courses.name: Phonological Theory
courses.description: Phonological rules, representations, and optimality theory.

courses.description: Spatial statistics, cartography, and GIS fundamentals.

courses.description: Kinship, religion, politics, and symbolic systems across cultures.
